title: Ervum paucifolium
intro: Ervum paucifolium, also known as small-leaved vetchling, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include the relief of fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ervum paucifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Ervum paucifolium include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Ervum paucifolium is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Vicieae. The plant is further grouped into the genus Ervum and the order Fabales. The specific classification of Ervum paucifolium is subject to ongoing research and updates in botanical taxonomy. More information is needed to provide a comprehensive classification of this species.
